---
title: Microsoft Entra ID — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: entra-id
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: active
phase: phase-1
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-04-15'
---

# Microsoft Entra ID — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `active` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-1`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Microsoft Entra ID Adapter is the **identity-fabric foundation** of the UIAO adapter ecosystem. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it consumes Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) data via the Microsoft Graph API to establish and maintain canonical object identity for every tenant, policy, control, and evidence artifact the substrate produces.

This is the adapter that satisfies the UIAO Adapter Doctrine's core mandate: *identity ensures every transaction is certificate-anchored*. All other Microsoft-stack adapters — dynamic groups, administrative units, device OrgPath, policy targeting, SCuBA / ScubaGear — depend on the identity graph that this adapter produces.

Three primary functions:

1. **Identity inventory.** Enumerates user objects, group objects, service principals, and managed identities via Microsoft Graph, producing one `ClaimObject` per principal with full Graph attribute preservation.
2. **Conditional Access assessment.** Reads conditional access policies, named locations, authentication methods policy, and directory role assignments, producing the canonical posture record used by the [`scubagear`](../scubagear/scubagear.html) baseline assessment.
3. **OrgPath synchronization sink.** Receives OrgPath values propagated from on-premises via Entra Connect sync (written by the [`active-directory`](../active-directory/active-directory.html) adapter) and confirms their presence in the cloud directory so downstream OrgPath consumers (dynamic groups, admin units, device tagging, policy targeting) can resolve them.

**Implementation:** `src/uiao/adapters/entra_adapter.py` + `src/uiao/collectors/entra/entra_collector.py`. The collector is a real `httpx`-based Graph API client (not a stub). Conformance: 30/30 PASS across the canonical adapter test matrix.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `user-objects`, `group-objects`, `service-principals`, `conditional-access-policies`

**Reads:** Microsoft Graph endpoints for users, groups, service principals, conditional access policies, named locations, directory roles, authentication methods policy, and organization metadata. Auth via client credentials flow (app registration with the documented least-privilege scopes).

**Emits:** `ClaimSet` with identity-rooted claims (one claim per principal), `EvidenceObject` with KSI-anchored provenance per ADR-006, plus the `migration-manifest.json` and `migration-audit.json` artifacts that anchor cross-adapter correlation back to canonical identity.

**Does NOT:** modify Entra ID configuration (conditional access, named locations, directory roles), access user credentials or refresh tokens, process person identity (object identity only per the canon invariant), or write to Graph endpoints outside the documented modernization workflow paths.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CM-8`, `IA-2`, `IA-4`, `AC-2`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Primary | Enumerates all identity objects (users, groups, service principals, managed identities) as canonical claims with stable Graph object identifiers. |
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Primary | Conditional Access assessment verifies MFA enforcement, sign-in conditions, and named-location policy across the tenant. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | Service principal and app registration inventory ensures every workload identifier is tracked and correlatable across the substrate. |
| **AC-2** Account Management | Supporting | User and group enumeration supports account lifecycle verification and feeds OrgPath-targeted dynamic groups via UIAO_152. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `powershell-7.4` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Modernization adapter — performs change-making actions against identity systems (user / group / service-principal provisioning, conditional-access policy reads). Classified per resolved ODA-15 (UIAO_003 §4.7).

Pairs upstream with the [`active-directory`](../active-directory/active-directory.html) adapter (which writes OrgPath into on-prem extension attributes); OrgPath values flow into Entra via Entra Connect sync rather than direct Graph writes from this adapter. Pairs downstream with [`entra-dynamic-groups`](../entra-dynamic-groups/entra-dynamic-groups.html), [`entra-admin-units`](../entra-admin-units/entra-admin-units.html), [`entra-device-orgpath`](../entra-device-orgpath/entra-device-orgpath.html), and [`entra-policy-targeting`](../entra-policy-targeting/entra-policy-targeting.html), all of which read the identity graph this adapter publishes.

ZTMM pillars: identity, devices, applications-and-workloads.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-002
- UIAO-CANON-003
- UIAO_007 — OrgTree Modernization: AD to Entra ID
- UIAO_009 — Microsoft Coverage and Gap Doctrine

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
